name: Aggregated EHS Component Calculation and Reporting
description:
This task requires you to interpret a real-world chemical inventory scenario involving multiple tanks of an acid solution, calculate the aggregated weight of the pure Extremely Hazardous Substance (EHS) component, and enter it into EPA Tier2 Submit.

Goal: Calculate the total pure Nitric Acid weight from two 35% solution tanks, add it as a single 'Pure' chemical entry with an exact amount of 3,500 lbs and two attached storage locations, update the facility's NAICS code, and save the finalized submission.

Starting State: EPA Tier2 Submit 2025 is running. A partially completed submission file (C:\workspace\data\precision_aerospace_base.t2s) is pre-loaded (or copied to your Desktop\Tier2Tasks directory). The facility 'Precision Aerospace Components' exists but has a blank NAICS code and an empty chemical inventory.

Expected Actions:
1. Calculate pure EHS component weights based on the facility physical inventory: Tank A has 6,000 lbs of 35% Nitric Acid solution, Tank B has 4,000 lbs of 35% Nitric Acid solution.
2. Navigate to the Facility tab and enter the primary NAICS code: 336412 (Aircraft Engine and Engine Parts Manufacturing).
3. Add a new chemical entry for the calculated EHS component: Nitric Acid (CAS 7697-37-2), marked as 'Pure', EHS='Yes', Physical State='Liquid'.
4. Select hazards: Oxidizer, Corrosive to metal, Acute Toxicity (Immediate), and Skin Corrosion.
5. Bypass range codes and use the 'Exact Amount' fields: Maximum Daily Amount = 3500 lbs, Average Daily Amount = 3500 lbs, Days on Site = 365.
6. Add two distinct storage location records under this single chemical entry: Location 1 ('Passivation Line 1 - Tank A', Vat or Open Tank, Ambient Temp/Pressure) and Location 2 ('Passivation Line 2 - Tank B', Vat or Open Tank, Ambient Temp/Pressure).
7. Save the finalized file to C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\Tier2Output\precision_aerospace_final.t2s.
